refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to ensure packet delivery.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.

potential_sources: Interaction between adaptive bitrate control and teleoperation command latency.
Dependency on network link quality and variability.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality control, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that adaptive algorithms may introduce latency affecting operator control.
Risk of insufficient error correction under extreme network degradation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating fallback mechanisms or redundant channels if adaptive bitrate alone is insufficient.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware components responsible for teleoperation communication and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion risk if DP implemented here due to broad scope and multiple responsibilities.
Low containment relevance; vehicle hardware is not responsible for communication quality control.
High containment and low diffusion scores; best fit for implementing communication quality control.
Limited relevance; safety services may consume alerts but do not control communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Implementing the DP within the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality control, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link stability outside Teleoperation control.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface definitions and monitoring hooks for Safety Services to receive timely alerts.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive bitrate and error correction) directly addresses the refined FR with measurable performance criteria and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Telemetry.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface during teleoperation tests.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback communication strategies in DP to handle extreme network conditions.
Define explicit alerting protocols to Safety Services for communication degradation.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. All gates pass with manageable risks and clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; no immediate revision needed.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Adaptive bitrate algorithms can meet packet loss targets without excessive latency.
open_risks: Potential latency impact on operator control.
Extreme network degradation scenarios not fully mitigated.
evidence_needed: Performance test results of adaptive bitrate and error correction under varying network conditions.
Verification of interface robustness between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
